This is an example of a simple Parsons Problem without any "faded" inputs to fill. Conceptually, this question is very similar to one that uses `pl-order-blocks`, with the difference that `pl-faded-parsons` runs the [standard python autograder](https://docs.prairielearn.com/python-grader/#overview) and uses a test suited to determine if a submission is correct. This allows questions with a wide range of possible solutions. As long as the student's solution passes the same test suite as the provided correct solution, it gets full credit! To solve this question, drag code from the starter tray on the left to the solution tray on the right. Rearrange and re-indent the lines in the solution tray, and fill in any blanks. Then hit save and grade. Note that there is a red-herring "distractor" line! *Note: Keyboard controls are available and listed in the (?) button* def fibonacci(n: int): #0given if n <= 2: #1given return 1 #2given n_less_2 = fibonacci(n - 2) n_less_1 = fibonacci(n - 1) n_less_0 = fibonacci(n) #distractor return n_less_1 + n_less_2 #1given
